w20: Cost-efficient micro instance for minimal workloads, featuring shared CPU and limited memory, suitable for basic development and testing environments.
c150: Google Cloud Platform f1-micro: An entry-level, cost-optimized instance with shared x86_64 CPU and 1GB RAM, ideal for minimal, non-critical workloads.
c200: The Google Cloud Platform f1-micro offers a shared x86_64 Intel Xeon vCPU and 1GB RAM at a low cost. Its performance metrics indicate suitability for development, testing, and very light, non-production tasks.
w150: Google Cloud Platform's f1-micro is an entry-level, cost-optimized instance. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon CPU architecture, providing 1 virtual CPU on a shared physical core with 1 physical core and 1 thread per core. The server is equipped with 1 GB of memory and does not include any bundled GPU or local storage. Performance benchmarks indicate poor results across various metrics, including a stress-ng single-core CPU performance of 384.79 and multi-core CPU performance of 384.79, both categorized in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth for read-only small blocks is 80.8 GB/sec, and write large blocks is 0.2 GB/sec, also in the bottom 10%. The Passmark CPU score is 236.08, and Redis SET operations achieve 0.24 million operations/sec, reflecting limited processing capabilities. This server is suitable for highly cost-sensitive, non-demanding workloads such as basic development environments, testing, or extremely low-traffic web serving.
